codeReview.summary: The original hardening commit did not fully solve reviewed-scope semantics, but the later remediation commits now do: `ProcessRun` derives reviewed and deleted paths from the assembled request, threads them into persistence, and `transitionMissingFinding` uses that explicit scope to mark reviewed-clean files fixed, out-of-scope disappearances stale, and deleted-file disappearances fixed after canonical anchor normalization. The current targeted `internal/llm` coverage exercises reviewed-clean, mixed-scope stale, deleted-file, and reviewed-scope derivation cases, so the prior scrutiny failure is resolved.

summary: Reviewed the original feature handoff/transcript plus the remediation features `finding-reviewed-scope-persistence-and-missing-transitions` and `finding-engine-scrutiny-remediation-round-2`, inspected the relevant diffs and current `internal/llm` code, and confirmed the repository now persists real reviewed scope from assembled changes. Missing findings on reviewed-clean files transition to `fixed`, mixed-scope/out-of-scope disappearances transition to `stale`, deleted-file handling remains `fixed` after anchor canonicalization, and the prior scrutiny failure is now addressed.
